variables.USER_FOCUS: High-intensity adversarial review for a TypeScript Mantle L2 DeFi tooling monorepo with MCP, CLI, and unsigned transaction builders.
REPOSITORY_PROFILE: TypeScript npm workspace monorepo: packages/core, packages/cli, packages/mcp, plus docs and Mantle skill integrations.
Build and checks: npm run build, npm run typecheck, vitest unit tests, and vitest e2e tests.
Product surface: Mantle MCP server and CLI for AI agents to query Mantle L2 state and build DeFi operation payloads.
Critical safety contract: write tools return unsigned transaction payloads only; the repo must not hold private keys, sign transactions, or broadcast transactions.

Public contracts: MCP tool names and schemas, CLI flags and stdout/stderr behavior, package exports, address/token registry behavior, unsigned_tx shape {to, data, value, chainId}, and human_summary output.
DeFi integrations include Merchant Moe, Agni Finance, Fluxion, and Aave V3 on Mantle.

Main risk areas: transaction payload correctness, whitelist bypasses, unsafe spender/target addresses, token decimals, amount parsing, slippage and fee-tier handling, allowance flows, recipient mistakes, MNT gas token assumptions, and chain ID correctness.
External IO risk areas: Mantle RPC responses, subgraph/indexer SQL responses, token price data, protocol TVL data, endpoint health probes, retries, timeouts, and malformed provider responses.
Trust-boundary risk areas: MCP tool inputs/outputs, AI model generated arguments, file paths, environment variables, package exports, and npm-published package behavior.
Review should treat passing CI as necessary but not sufficient; adversarially verify safety invariants around transaction construction and protocol-specific assumptions.
